metadata.notes: Corrected after the 2026-07-15 rewind record: Kiro persists parent_session_id for at least rewind-born sessions, so the old universal no-parent-field assertion was false. The adapter does not consume that field and no live /spawn child has been captured, therefore orphan cleanup remains frozen.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: n/a

Kiro supports subagents, but this scenario needs a linked live child before a process-group kill can exercise orphan cleanup. The former assessment incorrectly claimed sidecars never carry a parent field. A live 2.6.0 /rewind record proves a rewind-born child sidecar has top-level parent_session_id.

That correction does not yet make the scenario recordable: no /spawn child has been recorded to establish that it writes the same field, and the current Kiro adapter reads sidecars only for cwd, never ParentSessionID. The daemon would consequently still see an unlinked child and could not test the parent/child cleanup invariant.

## Unblock

Record a real spawned child, verify its persisted parent edge, and implement sidecar parent projection in the adapter. Only then add the driver kill step and record the cleanup behavior.
caveats: Rewind proves schema capability, not /spawn parity.
The issue #321 cleanup invariant remains covered by adapters with observable links.
No existing fixture should assert that Kiro sidecars lack a parent field at every depth.

sources.note: Live rewind child sidecar with parent_session_id.
Only cwd is read from the sidecar.

recipe.note: Frozen until a spawned child edge is live-proven and projected by the adapter.
